title: Rich Malaysian White Fish Stir-fry
description: A rich and tender dish featuring white fish that is perfect for those looking for a high-protein-friendly meal.
instructions: Combine minced garlic and chili paste in a mixing bowl.
Add fish sauce, soy sauce, sugar, salt to the mix of garlic and chili paste. Mix well until all ingredients are combined and stir into a paste consistency.
Gently flake white fish into small pieces and set aside.
Heat vegetable oil in a wok or large frying pan over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the green onion slices, tomato slices, and mix of garlic/chili/fish sauce.
When vegetables are just starting to wilt, add the flaked white fish. Stir-fry for about 2-3 minutes until fish is coated in the mixture.
Add cornstarch (if using) and stir well until a thick sauce forms. Reduce heat if necessary to prevent burning. Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes or until the sauce thickens slightly.
Garnish with fresh green onions and serve hot.
